blockers.description: The question asks for "most popular posts", but popularity can be measured in multiple ways, such as highest score, highest view count, highest comment count.
example_questions: How should I measure the popularity of a post?
How do I determine if a post is popular?
What criteria defines that a post is popular?
blockers.resolution: A post is considered popular if it has a FavoriteCount of 2 or more.

blockers.description: The term "lengthy post" is not precisely defined. It is unclear what character or word count qualifies a post as lengthy.
example_questions: How long does a post need to be to be considered lengthy?
What is the minimum length for a post to be considered lengthy?
How do I identify that a post is considered lengthy?
blockers.resolution: A lengthy post is defined as one where the character count of the body exceeds 2,500 characters.

blockers.description: The phrase "first few years of the platform" is ambiguous, it could mean the first year only, the first two years, or the first three years or more.
example_questions: What time period does the "first few years of the platform" refer to?
What is the exact date range for the first few years of the platform?
Can you tell me how I should filter for the first few years of the platform?
blockers.resolution: The first few years of the platform refers strictly to posts with CreaionDate >= '2009-01-01' AND CreaionDate < '2011-01-01'

blockers.description: The business information defines "good badges" as those considered to be relevant to the platform's content and community standards, but the definition is vague and could map to many badges.
example_questions: Which specific badge qualifies as a "good badge"?
What badge name should I filter to identify users with good badges?
Can you clarify which badges are considered "good"?
blockers.resolution: Good badges are specifically those awarded for 'Custodian', 'Citizen Patrol', and 'Organizer', badges that recognize users who have maintained content quality on the platform.

blockers.description: The user_activity_stats table contains three columns that could all plausibly represent a user's activity percentage, with no clear indication of which one represents the activity percentage.
example_questions: How do I identify which column represents a user's average activity percentage?
Which column represents the user's average activity percentage?
What does each column in user_activity_stats represent?
blockers.resolution: The activity percentage must be taken from the reply_level column in the user_activity_stats table.
